title: AI-powered trading tools
author: maulikms
selftext: Has anyone else been experimenting with AI-assisted strategies to manage high-leverage positions, such as shorting stocks like NVIDIA? I kept blowing stops on crypto because I was guessing momentum incorrectly, so I started using AI signal tools combined with a faster execution platform. When the AI flagged a reversal, I needed a way to close positions quickly. While it's not a magic fix, pairing smarter signals with quick execution has genuinely helped reduce my revenge-trading impulses. I'm curious to hear what setups others are running, especially regarding stocks!

comments.author: Beautiful-Town2909
comments.body: I recently got a 312% on $MU which was great. 5X leverage, took me about 2 months. I’m currently still open 5X on $SNDK and $WDC, up 80% and 172% respectively. My current setup is using StockGPT, and the portfolio it made me, and then just leveraging the hell outta it. Probably not meant to but it’s working!! Other than that, r/ai_trading of course. The holy grail. When it’s not filled with bots. Or scams. What AI signal tools are you using? Please let me know, always looking for new ideas. I’ll drop the link to StockGPT, working really well for me so far. www.stockgpt.pro

comments.author: AttitudeGrouchy33
comments.body:
I’m the founder of milo, so I’m biased here, but this matches what we’ve learned building around trading agents: the signal is only one piece.

The part that actually changes behavior is the loop around the signal:

- what position am I already in?
- how much risk am I allowed to take?
- what would invalidate the trade?
- what happens if volatility spikes?
- can I exit without hesitating?

A lot of “AI trading” tools stop at prediction. That’s useful, but it can also make people overconfident. The more interesting direction is agents with fixed playbooks, explicit risk limits, and a record you can inspect after every move.

We’re building in that direction at milo: https://app.andmilo.com/?code=@milo4reddit

My main lesson so far: AI should reduce revenge trading and hesitation, not give people a new excuse to over-leverage.

comments.author: Artistic-East-1251
comments.body: yeah same issue with crypto momentum, was always a step behind. Started using co-vest from liquid for the quick closes when signals flip, the instant exit made a real difference in cutting losses before they snowballed.

replies.author: Lisat_Cruzl
replies.body: Totally agree, 've been using co-Invest too, and the biggest benefit for me has been the speed of execution when conditions change
